mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, invalidating the labor market deterioration signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based collapse in consumer demand and credit-dependent sectors, producing negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods yield positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, refuting the broad demand collapse mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side resilience, which predict positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during identified recovery regimes, or returns are negative in over 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation exceeding 0.8% month-over-month forces expectations of tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate earnings, generating negative forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months produce positive returns in more than 50% of instances, contradicting the stagflation risk channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate, where payrolls decline while unemployment rises, signals structural labor market weakness that predicts negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Divergence periods show positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, invalidating the structural weakness hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects strengthening economic fundamentals and corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth periods yield negative forward returns in more than 60% of instances, breaking the fundamentals-to-equity transmission.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the industrial production index below 105 indicates severe supply-side contraction and reduced economic activity, triggering negative short-term market returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the threshold is breached, refuting the supply contraction signal.
